title: azalea irrorata
intro: Azalea irrorata, also known as the "dotted azalea" or "dot azalea," is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. The leaves and roots of the Azalea irrorata plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and improving respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Azalea irrorata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to treat various health conditions.
